content:
Now that Rust enablement for AArch64 is merged I wonder what are the next steps?

If I understood correctly it is not possible to get anything like abstractions merged without any "user". And this "user" has to be "new". It shall not be an existing C driver or similar just ported to Rust.

Or course we have the binder project. But what else?

In Possible project: IIO drivers Trevor mentioned that IIO might be a good starting point.

What I really like is @Fabien Parent's ncv6336 regulator driver. It would need device, platform, io_pgtable, basic of/devicetree, i2c, regmap and regulator abstractions, though. But most probably most of this would be needed for IIO via I2C, as well.

What do you think?

I'm currently working on documenting/cleaning the code/tweaking the APIs here and there for the branch you linked. My plan is to get it ready pretty soon and submit it to the mailing list.
I hope that by pushing my patches to the mailing list I can also get things moving forward for the drivers I'm depending on such platform driver, and OF driver.

It shall not be an existing C driver or similar just ported to Rust.

It can be an existing C driver if the subsystem maintainers agree to have a "Rust reference driver" to bootstrap things (i.e. a typically small but well-known driver in their subsystem).

For instance, see the Asix PHY driver that is now on mainline:

config AX88796B_RUST_PHY
    bool "Rust reference driver for Asix PHYs"
    depends on RUST_PHYLIB_ABSTRACTIONS && AX88796B_PHY
    help
      Uses the Rust reference driver for Asix PHYs (ax88796b_rust.ko).
      The features are equivalent. It supports the Asix Electronics PHY
      found in the X-Surf 100 AX88796B package.

It allows you to switch (at build time) between the C or the Rust driver.

To be clear, that kind of exceptional driver is an exceptional case and is only meant for bootstrapping -- duplicate code is not allowed in the kernel in general, so one of the two drivers should eventually go away.

I will a note about this to the webpage.
If you meant something for aarch64 specifically, I know there was a lot of interest from netdev in getting aarch64 BE support for rust merged too, just to have a BE target

I will a note about this to the webpage.

Done: https://rust-for-linux.com/rust-reference-drivers
What about @Asahi Lina’s GPU driver?

I don't think that was blocked only on aarch64 build config, there is still a ton to be upstreamed before that can happen. It sounds like Lina has been busy https://rust-for-linux.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/288089-General/topic/Rust.20M1.20DRM.20driver.20runs.20GNOME!/near/390581894, but I think @Maíra Canal is progressing on some of the dependencies
